# Expo SDK 54 → 56 Upgrade Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Upgrade the `mobile/` app from Expo SDK 54 (RN 0.81 / React 19.1) to SDK 56 (RN 0.85 / React 19.2) at behavior parity, then adopt type-safe config plugins, new Expo Router APIs, and Expo UI components — in one branch, sequenced migration-first.

**Architecture:** CNG workflow (native dirs regenerated by `expo prebuild --clean`; native tweaks via config plugins). In-place direct upgrade. Each phase ends green (`npm test` + `npx tsc --noEmit` + Android boot/smoke of touched flows) before the next. iOS verified via EAS at the end. Guiding principle: **use Expo/RN APIs as intended — no workarounds, no legacy bridges unless an API genuinely has no modern equivalent.**

**Tech Stack:** Expo SDK 56, React Native 0.85, React 19.2, Expo Router (no react-navigation), expo-file-system (new `File`/`Directory` API), Expo UI (`@expo/ui`), expo-sqlite, jest-expo, EAS.

**Branch:** `sdk-56-upgrade` (already checked out, based on `feat/asset-improvements`).

## Progress

- **Phase 1 — DONE & Android-verified.** SDK 56 installed/aligned; `tsc` clean; 104/104 tests pass; release APK builds (JDK 17) and runs on the emulator (clean boot, tab nav, coin feature renders). Migrations: removed `@react-native-community/datetimepicker` → `@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker`; `useBackup.ts` + `settings.tsx` → new `File`/`Paths` FS API; `absoluteFillObject`→`absoluteFill`; `global`→`globalThis` in tests; dropped redundant reanimated babel plugin.
- **Phase 2 — DONE (Android-verified; iOS pending Phase 5).** `withPrivacyManifest` → built-in `ios.privacyManifests` config (plugin deleted, config resolves). `withLocalizedAppName` and `withAppDelegatePublicAccess` → converted to typed `ConfigPlugin` `.ts` (both kept: `locales` does not cover display-name/`app_name`; AppDelegate plugin retained pending the EAS iOS build to confirm whether SDK 56's Swift template still emits `public`). Added `@types/node` + `tsconfig types:["jest","node"]` for build-time plugins. Prebuild clean, localized strings generated.
- **Phase 3 — DONE.** No `@react-navigation/*` usage anywhere (not in code, package.json, or node_modules) → the replacement codemod is a no-op; the app already uses pure expo-router primitives. New SDK 56 Router APIs (`Stack.Toolbar`, `SuspenseFallback`, server/static loaders, `generateMetadata`/SSR) don't fit a headerless offline-first mobile app → not adopted (YAGNI). Cleanup: removed two stale `as never` route casts in `app/_layout.tsx` (typedRoutes now validates `/(tabs)` and `/(onboarding)`). `tsc` clean, 104/104 tests pass.
- **Phase 4 — DONE.** Date picker already on `@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker` (Phase 1); modernized its deprecated `onChange` → `onValueChange`. Evaluated `@expo/ui/community/picker` for the 5 `PickerSheet`/`PickerModal` selectors and **kept the custom components** — the native wheel picker would lose the themed bottom sheet, selection checkmarks, safe-area handling, and the two-step country→coin + "add own coin" (`__add__`) flow they implement (YAGNI / no regression). `tsc` clean, 104/104 tests pass.
- **Phase 5 — next.** EAS iOS verification (build, smoke-test, confirm the two retained iOS-native plugins + privacy manifest + localized display name).

**KNOWN UPSTREAM ISSUE (Android dev client):** Expo SDK 56's Android **dev client** crashes on cold start with `ReferenceError: Property 'MessageQueue' doesn't exist` / `Global was not installed`, thrown from the `__DEV__` devtools init path (`setUpReactDevTools`/`LogBox`) under bridgeless mode — see [expo/expo#46162](https://github.com/expo/expo/issues/46162). This is **NOT caused by this migration**: it affects the Android dev client only; **iOS dev client and Android release builds are unaffected**. Therefore Android verification for this upgrade uses a **release build** (`npx expo run:android --variant release`), not the dev client, until Expo ships a fix. Re-test the dev client after upgrading `expo`/`@expo/metro-runtime` patch releases.

**LOCAL BUILD ENV REQUIREMENT (JDK 17):** Local native Gradle/CMake builds MUST run on **JDK 17**. On JDK 24/25 the CMake configure subprocess prints `WARNING: A restricted method in java.lang.System has been called` to stderr (new JDK native-access restriction), and AGP's `GeneratePrefabPackages` reporter promotes that stderr line to a fatal `IllegalStateException`, failing `:react-native-worklets`/`:react-native-screens` `configureCMakeRelWithDebInfo`. Fix: build with `JAVA_HOME` pointing at a JDK 17 (e.g. the bundled `~/.gradle/jdks/eclipse_adoptium-17-*`) or pass `-Dorg.gradle.java.home=<jdk17>`. EAS cloud builds are unaffected (they pin their own JDK). Not a code issue.

**Source of truth for APIs:** verified against the official Expo SDK 56 changelog and docs (fetched 2026-06-01). Key facts used below:
- New FS API: `import { File, Directory, Paths } from 'expo-file-system'`; `new File(Paths.document, name)`, `await file.text()`, `file.write(str)`, `file.exists`, `file.delete()`. Legacy lives at `expo-file-system/legacy`.
- `expo/fetch` is now the default `globalThis.fetch` (opt out via `EXPO_PUBLIC_USE_RN_FETCH=1`).
- `expo` no longer auto-depends on `@expo/vector-icons` (we already list it explicitly — keep it).
- Status/nav bar: new `<StatusBar>` (expo-status-bar) and `<NavigationBar>` (expo-navigation-bar) components.
- Router: `expo-router` no longer depends on react-navigation; codemod `npx expo-codemod sdk-56-expo-router-react-navigation-replace <dir>`.
- Expo UI drop-ins: `import DateTimePicker from '@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker'`, `import { Picker } from '@expo/ui/community/picker'`.

---

## File Structure (what this plan touches)

**Modify:**
- `mobile/package.json` — SDK 56 dep versions (most via `expo install --fix`).
- `mobile/lib/hooks/useBackup.ts` — migrate the two legacy FS I/O spots to the new `File` API (preserve the existing hook shape/behavior).
- `mobile/app/_layout.tsx` — verify only; `<StatusBar>` is unchanged in SDK 56 (no nav-bar code exists here).
- `mobile/app/(tabs)/assets.tsx` — DateTimePicker → `@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker` (Phase 4).
- `mobile/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`, `app/(tabs)/index.tsx`, `app/(onboarding)/_layout.tsx`, `app/(onboarding)/index.tsx` — router codemod output (Phase 3, if any react-navigation usage exists).
- `mobile/plugins/*.js` — evaluate/replace/convert (Phase 2).
- `mobile/app.json` — config-plugin / status-bar / privacy-manifest / locales config (Phases 2–3).
- `mobile/__mocks__/expo-sqlite.ts`, jest config — jest-expo 56 fixes (Phase 1).

**Possibly delete:** `mobile/plugins/withAppDelegatePublicAccess.js`, `withLocalizedAppName.js`, `withPrivacyManifest.js` (Phase 2, if built-in config covers them).

---

## Phase 1 — Core SDK bump to parity (THE GATE)

Nothing in Phases 2–5 starts until Phase 1's exit criteria pass.

### Task 1.1: Baseline + branch hygiene

**Files:** none (verification only)

- [ ] **Step 1: Confirm green baseline on SDK 54**

Run: `cd mobile && npm test`
Expected: all suites pass (current: ~15 suites). Record the count.

- [ ] **Step 2: Confirm clean working tree**

Run: `git status --short`
Expected: empty (all prior work committed).

### Task 1.2: Bump Expo to SDK 56 and align dependencies

**Files:** Modify `mobile/package.json` (+ `package-lock.json`)

- [ ] **Step 1: Install SDK 56**

Run: `cd mobile && npm install expo@^56.0.0`
Expected: installs; peer warnings are OK at this stage.

- [ ] **Step 2: Align all dependencies to the SDK**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo install --fix`
Expected: bumps react-native→0.85.x, react/react-dom→19.2.x, all `expo-*`→56.x, reanimated/gesture-handler/screens/safe-area/svg/datetimepicker, jest-expo→56.x, etc. Re-run once and confirm it reports nothing left to fix.

- [ ] **Step 3: Ensure `@expo/vector-icons` is still present and aligned**

The app uses `@expo/vector-icons` (Ionicons) and `expo` no longer pulls it transitively. Confirm it remains a direct dependency and is on the version `expo install` expects:
Run: `cd mobile && npx expo install @expo/vector-icons`
Expected: installed at the SDK-56-compatible version.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the dependency bump**

```bash
cd mobile && git add package.json package-lock.json && git commit -m "chore: bump Expo to SDK 56 and align dependencies"
```

### Task 1.3: Regenerate native projects and run doctor

**Files:** none committed (android/ios are gitignored)

- [ ] **Step 1: Clean regenerate Android native project**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo prebuild --clean --platform android`
Expected: regenerates `android/` from config + plugins. If a config plugin throws, note which one (handled in Phase 2) — for now the prebuild must complete; if a plugin breaks prebuild, temporarily comment it out in `app.json` plugins and record it as a Phase 2 must-fix.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run expo-doctor**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo-doctor`
Expected: lists any remaining issues. Resolve dependency/version issues it reports (most are fixed by re-running `npx expo install --fix`). Record anything it can't auto-resolve.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit any package.json changes from doctor**

```bash
cd mobile && git add package.json package-lock.json app.json && git commit -m "chore: resolve expo-doctor findings for SDK 56" || echo "nothing to commit"
```

### Task 1.4: Migrate expo-file-system to the new `File`/`Directory` API

**Files:** Modify `mobile/lib/hooks/useBackup.ts`

Context (verified against the actual file): the hook is well-formed — it already obtains `db` via `useSQLiteContext()`, has a `BackupStatus` state machine, and exposes `{ status, handleExport, handleImport, handleClear }` with signature `useBackup(onDataChanged?)`. It uses two legacy FS calls: `FileSystem.cacheDirectory` + `FileSystem.writeAsStringAsync(...)` in `handleExport`, and reads the picked import file via `fetch(uri).text()` in `handleImport`. **Only those two I/O spots change.** Preserve the status machine, `handleClear`, `clearAllData`/`applySystemDefaults`, the signature, and the return shape exactly. (There is NO `db` bug — do not change how `db` is obtained.)

- [ ] **Step 1: Swap the import**

In `mobile/lib/hooks/useBackup.ts`, replace:
```ts
import * as FileSystem from 'expo-file-system';
```
with:
```ts
import { File, Paths } from 'expo-file-system';
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Migrate the export write to the new API**

In `handleExport`, replace the legacy write block:
```ts
        const path = `${FileSystem.cacheDirectory ?? ''}oam_backup_${ts}.json`;
        await FileSystem.writeAsStringAsync(path, json, { encoding: FileSystem.EncodingType.UTF8 });
        await Sharing.shareAsync(path, {
          mimeType:    'application/json',
          dialogTitle: 'Vaultic Backup',
          UTI:         'public.json',
        });
```
with:
```ts
        const file = new File(Paths.cache, `oam_backup_${ts}.json`);
        if (file.exists) file.delete();
        file.create();
        file.write(json);
        await Sharing.shareAsync(file.uri, {
          mimeType:    'application/json',
          dialogTitle: 'Vaultic Backup',
          UTI:         'public.json',
        });
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Migrate the import read to the new API**

In `handleImport`, replace:
```ts
      const res  = await fetch(result.assets[0].uri);
      const json = await res.text();
```
with:
```ts
      const json = await new File(result.assets[0].uri).text();
```
(Keep `copyToCacheDirectory: true` on the `DocumentPicker.getDocumentAsync` call above it.)

- [ ] **Step 4: Type-check**

Run: `cd mobile && npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: no errors in `useBackup.ts`. If `new File(<single string uri>)` is rejected by the SDK 56 types for a picked content URI, check `node_modules/expo-file-system` types for the correct single-URI reader; only if there is genuinely no new-API reader for a content URI, use `import { readAsStringAsync } from 'expo-file-system/legacy'` for that ONE read with a one-line justification comment (per the no-workarounds principle).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add lib/hooks/useBackup.ts && git commit -m "feat: migrate backup I/O to new expo-file-system File API"
```

### Task 1.5: Verify status bar (no nav-bar migration needed)

**Files:** `mobile/app/_layout.tsx` (verify; likely no change)

Context (verified against the actual file): `_layout.tsx` only renders `<StatusBar style={isDark ? 'light' : 'dark'} />` from `expo-status-bar`. There is **no** `expo-navigation-bar`, `expo-system-ui`, or imperative system-UI code in this file. The `<StatusBar>` component and its `style` prop are unchanged in SDK 56, so **no migration is required here**.

- [ ] **Step 1: Confirm the status bar still type-checks and renders**

Run: `cd mobile && npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: no errors involving `_layout.tsx`. During the Android smoke test (Task 1.7) confirm the status bar style flips correctly between light/dark themes.

- [ ] **Step 2: (Optional, only if a need exists) adopt `<NavigationBar>`**

The app does not currently manage the Android navigation bar. Do NOT add `expo-navigation-bar` unless the Android smoke test reveals a nav-bar contrast/edge-to-edge problem. If it does, add the declarative component:
```tsx
import { NavigationBar } from 'expo-navigation-bar';
// ...inside the render, next to <StatusBar/>:
<NavigationBar style="auto" />
```
and `npx expo install expo-navigation-bar`. Otherwise record "no nav-bar management needed (YAGNI)" and make no change.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit only if changed**

```bash
cd mobile && git add app/_layout.tsx package.json && git commit -m "feat: manage Android navigation bar via SDK 56 component" || echo "no change needed"
```

### Task 1.6: Fix the test harness for jest-expo 56

**Files:** Modify `mobile/__mocks__/expo-sqlite.ts` and/or `mobile/package.json` (jest config) as needed

- [ ] **Step 1: Run the suite and capture failures**

Run: `cd mobile && npm test 2>&1 | tail -40`
Expected: some failures are likely from jest-expo 56 / jest 30 (transform, setup, or the expo-sqlite mock). Record the actual errors.

- [ ] **Step 2: Fix the failures per the real errors**

Apply the minimal fixes the errors call for. Likely candidates:
- If `jest-expo` preset path or transformIgnorePatterns changed, update the jest config in `package.json` to match the jest-expo 56 preset.
- If `expo-sqlite` mock breaks because the module surface changed, update `__mocks__/expo-sqlite.ts` to match the SDK 56 `expo-sqlite` API used by the repositories.
- If `@testing-library/react-native` needs a version aligned to React 19.2, align it via `npm install`.
Do not weaken assertions to make tests pass — fix the setup.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run until green**

Run: `cd mobile && npm test`
Expected: all suites pass again (same count as the Phase 1.1 baseline, plus any tests you had to adjust for API shape — adjustments must preserve the original behavioral intent).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add __mocks__/expo-sqlite.ts package.json package-lock.json && git commit -m "test: fix harness for jest-expo 56"
```

### Task 1.7: Fix remaining type + runtime breakage; verify on Android

**Files:** wherever `tsc` / runtime points (e.g. reanimated 4.4, gesture-handler 3, async-storage 3, RN 0.85 type changes)

- [ ] **Step 1: Type-check the whole app**

Run: `cd mobile && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v "__mocks__\|__tests__" | head -40`
Expected: resolve every NEW error in `app/`/`lib/`. Common ones: RN 0.85 style/prop type tightening, reanimated 4.4 worklet/type changes, gesture-handler 3 import changes. Fix each per the library's SDK 56 guidance. (async-storage is only used for the one-time migration — confirm its import still resolves under v3.)

- [ ] **Step 2: Boot on Android and smoke-test**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo run:android` (or `eas build --profile development --platform android` if no local emulator, then install).
Smoke-test every core flow: onboarding, dashboard (live prices fetch — confirms `expo/fetch` default works), assets list + add a coin (catalog picker, count, alloy/weight), zakat, debts, settings, backup **export AND import** (now that the `db` bug is fixed), app-lock, language switch (incl. an RTL language).
Expected: all work. If price/FX fetch fails, test with `EXPO_PUBLIC_USE_RN_FETCH=1` in `.env` to isolate the `expo/fetch` change and record findings.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run the web E2E suite as an extra signal**

Run: `cd mobile && npm run test:e2e` (Playwright). Expected: pass, or triage any failures caused by the upgrade.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit any fixes**

```bash
cd mobile && git add -A && git commit -m "fix: resolve SDK 56 type/runtime breakage; Android parity verified"
```

**PHASE 1 EXIT CRITERIA (gate):** `npm test` green, `npx tsc --noEmit` clean (no new app/lib errors), app boots and all core flows work on Android, backup export+import works. Do not start Phase 2 until all four hold.

## Phase 2 — Config plugins: prefer built-in config, then type-safe for the rest

Goal: eliminate each custom plugin via native Expo config where possible; convert any that must remain to a typed `ConfigPlugin`.

### Task 2.1: Re-evaluate `withAppDelegatePublicAccess`

**Files:** possibly delete `mobile/plugins/withAppDelegatePublicAccess.js`; Modify `mobile/app.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Determine if it's still needed**

This plugin made AppDelegate symbols public for Xcode 26 (commit `80d8c1e`). On SDK 56, run a clean iOS prebuild and inspect the generated AppDelegate:
Run: `cd mobile && npx expo prebuild --clean --platform ios`
Then inspect `ios/*/AppDelegate.swift` for the access-level issue the plugin patched. If SDK 56 already generates it correctly, the plugin is obsolete.

- [ ] **Step 2: Remove it if obsolete**

If obsolete: delete `mobile/plugins/withAppDelegatePublicAccess.js` and remove `"./plugins/withAppDelegatePublicAccess"` from the `plugins` array in `mobile/app.json`. Re-run `npx expo prebuild --clean --platform ios` and confirm it still generates cleanly.
If still needed: convert it to TypeScript with a typed `ConfigPlugin` signature in Step (Task 2.4).

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add app.json plugins/ && git commit -m "chore: drop withAppDelegatePublicAccess (obsolete on SDK 56)" || echo "kept; handled in 2.4"
```

### Task 2.2: Replace `withLocalizedAppName` with built-in config

**Files:** Modify `mobile/app.json`; possibly delete `mobile/plugins/withLocalizedAppName.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Read what the plugin does**

Open `mobile/plugins/withLocalizedAppName.js` and note exactly which per-locale display names it injects (it relates to the 16 `CFBundleLocalizations` already in `app.json`).

- [ ] **Step 2: Move it to Expo's built-in `locales` config**

Expo supports per-locale strings (incl. `CFBundleDisplayName`) via the `locales` field in app config, pointing at per-language JSON files. Create `mobile/locales/<lang>.json` files with the localized `CFBundleDisplayName`, and add to `app.json`:
```json
"locales": {
  "en": "./locales/en.json",
  "de": "./locales/de.json"
}
```
(one entry per language the plugin localized). Confirm against the current Expo "App icons & localization / `locales`" docs at execution time for the exact JSON key for the display name.

- [ ] **Step 3: Remove the plugin and verify**

Delete `mobile/plugins/withLocalizedAppName.js`, remove it from `app.json` `plugins`, run `npx expo prebuild --clean --platform ios`, and confirm the generated `ios` project carries the localized display names (checked again on the EAS build in Phase 5).
If `locales` cannot express what the plugin did, keep the plugin and convert it in Task 2.4 instead.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add app.json locales/ plugins/ && git commit -m "chore: replace withLocalizedAppName with built-in locales config"
```

### Task 2.3: Replace `withPrivacyManifest` with built-in config if available

**Files:** Modify `mobile/app.json`; possibly delete `mobile/plugins/withPrivacyManifest.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Read the plugin**

Open `mobile/plugins/withPrivacyManifest.js` and note the `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` contents it injects (API-usage reasons etc.).

- [ ] **Step 2: Check for native config support**

Check whether SDK 56 app config supports `ios.privacyManifests` (the changelog did not announce a change, so this must be verified against current docs at execution). If supported, move the manifest into `app.json` under `ios.privacyManifests` and delete the plugin. If not supported, **keep** the plugin and convert it to a typed plugin in Task 2.4 (this is a legitimate "no built-in equivalent" case).

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify + commit**

Run `npx expo prebuild --clean --platform ios`; confirm `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` exists in the generated project.
```bash
cd mobile && git add app.json plugins/ && git commit -m "chore: move privacy manifest to built-in config (or retain plugin)"
```

### Task 2.4: Convert any retained plugins to typed ConfigPlugin (.ts)

**Files:** Modify retained files under `mobile/plugins/` (rename `.js`→`.ts`), update references in `app.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Convert**

For each plugin that survived Tasks 2.1–2.3, rename to `.ts` and type it:
```ts
import { ConfigPlugin } from 'expo/config-plugins';

const withX: ConfigPlugin = (config) => {
  // ...existing logic, now typed...
  return config;
};

export default withX;
```
Update the `plugins` reference in `app.json` if the path/extension changed (Expo resolves `./plugins/withX` to the `.ts`).

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify prebuild + Android still green**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo prebuild --clean --platform android && npm test && npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: prebuild succeeds, tests pass, types clean.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add plugins/ app.json && git commit -m "refactor: convert retained config plugins to typed ConfigPlugin"
```

## Phase 3 — Expo Router new features

### Task 3.1: Run the react-navigation replacement codemod

**Files:** Modify any file importing from `@react-navigation/*`

- [ ] **Step 1: Find react-navigation usage**

Run: `cd mobile && grep -rn "@react-navigation" app lib 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules`
Expected: list usages (may be none — the app uses expo-router's own APIs). If none, this codemod is a no-op; skip to Task 3.2.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the codemod (if usages exist)**

Run: `cd mobile && npx expo-codemod sdk-56-expo-router-react-navigation-replace app lib`
Expected: rewrites `@react-navigation/*` imports to expo-router equivalents. Review the diff.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify + commit**

Run: `cd mobile && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test`
```bash
cd mobile && git add -A && git commit -m "refactor: replace react-navigation usage via expo-router codemod" || echo "no usage; nothing to commit"
```

### Task 3.2: Adopt high-value new Router APIs (parity-preserving)

**Files:** Modify `mobile/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`, `mobile/app/_layout.tsx` as applicable

- [ ] **Step 1: Evaluate `Stack.Toolbar` and SuspenseFallback**

SDK 56 adds `Stack.Toolbar` (cross-platform toolbar) and customizable `SuspenseFallback` in `_layout`. Decide, per the current router docs, whether either improves the existing stack/tabs without changing behavior. If neither adds clear value, record "no change — existing Router primitives already idiomatic" and skip. (YAGNI: do not adopt features that don't serve a need.)

- [ ] **Step 2: If adopting, implement the smallest version + verify navigation**

Apply the chosen API; verify tabs + onboarding navigation on Android.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add -A && git commit -m "feat: adopt new Expo Router APIs where they add value" || echo "no change"
```

## Phase 4 — Expo UI adoption

### Task 4.1: Replace the date picker with the Expo UI drop-in

**Files:** Modify `mobile/app/(tabs)/assets.tsx`

Context: `assets.tsx` imports `DateTimePicker from '@react-native-community/datetimepicker'` and renders it for iOS (inline) and Android (dialog). SDK 56 provides a drop-in at `@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Swap the import**

In `mobile/app/(tabs)/assets.tsx`, change:
```tsx
import DateTimePicker from '@react-native-community/datetimepicker';
```
to:
```tsx
import DateTimePicker from '@expo/ui/community/datetime-picker';
```
Keep the existing usage/props the same (it is a drop-in). Ensure `@expo/ui` is installed: `cd mobile && npx expo install @expo/ui`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the date field still works**

Run: `cd mobile && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test`, then on Android open Add-Asset → date field → pick a past date → confirm it sets correctly (iOS inline vs Android dialog behavior preserved). If a prop differs from the community component, adjust per the `@expo/ui` datetime-picker docs (keep behavior identical).

- [ ] **Step 3: Remove the old dependency if fully unused**

Run: `cd mobile && grep -rn "@react-native-community/datetimepicker" app lib | grep -v node_modules`
If no matches: `cd mobile && npm uninstall @react-native-community/datetimepicker`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add -A && git commit -m "feat: adopt Expo UI datetime-picker drop-in"
```

### Task 4.2: Evaluate Expo UI for the custom Picker/BottomSheet (bounded)

**Files:** Modify `mobile/app/(tabs)/assets.tsx`, `mobile/lib/components/BottomSheet.tsx` (only if a clear win)

- [ ] **Step 1: Decide scope**

The app uses a custom `PickerSheet`/`BottomSheet` (not `@react-native-picker/picker` or `@gorhom/bottom-sheet`, which are not deps). Per the spec, adopt Expo UI native components ONLY where they clearly improve the existing UX. Evaluate `@expo/ui/community/picker` for the category/coin/currency selectors. If the native picker degrades the current grouped/search UX, **keep the custom component** and record the decision (YAGNI / no regressions).

- [ ] **Step 2: If adopting, do it for ONE selector first + verify**

Convert a single selector to `@expo/ui/community/picker`, verify on Android, then decide whether to roll out further. Keep value/onChange semantics identical.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
cd mobile && git add -A && git commit -m "feat: adopt Expo UI picker where it improves UX" || echo "kept custom components by decision"
```

---

## Phase 5 — iOS verification (EAS)

### Task 5.1: iOS build + smoke test via EAS

**Files:** none (build/verify)

- [ ] **Step 1: Development (simulator) build**

Run: `cd mobile && eas build --profile development --platform ios`
Expected: build succeeds on the EAS macOS image. If a config-plugin change (Phase 2) broke iOS generation, fix it now and rebuild.

- [ ] **Step 2: Smoke-test on iOS**

Install the simulator build; smoke-test the same flows as Task 1.7 Step 2, PLUS the Phase 2 iOS-sensitive items: **app display name per locale** (switch device language), **privacy manifest present** (Settings/app metadata), Face ID app-lock, share-sheet backup export, document-picker import.

- [ ] **Step 3: Preview build for both platforms**

Run: `cd mobile && eas build --profile preview --platform all`
Expected: both succeed. This is the release-candidate verification.

- [ ] **Step 4: Record results**

Append an "iOS verification" note (build IDs + smoke results) to the PR description / a short `docs/superpowers/` note. No code commit required unless fixes were made.

---

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** Phase 1 = SDK bump + breaking APIs (file-system, status/nav bar, fetch, tests, types) → gate. Phase 2 = config plugins (built-in-first, then typed). Phase 3 = Router (codemod + new APIs). Phase 4 = Expo UI (datetime-picker drop-in; bounded picker eval). Phase 5 = EAS iOS. Guiding principle (idiomatic/no-workarounds) is enforced per task (new FS API not legacy; remove plugins for built-in config; codemod for router; drop-ins for components).
- **Grounded in real docs:** file-system, status/nav bar, router codemod, and Expo UI import paths are taken from the fetched SDK 56 changelog/docs. Items that genuinely depend on runtime output (which deps/tests break under RN 0.85 / jest-expo 56) are written as "run → observe → fix per the documented change → verify," which is the honest shape for a migration; each still has a concrete verification gate.
- **Correction applied:** Tasks 1.4 and 1.5 were rewritten against the ACTUAL files — `useBackup.ts` has no `db` bug (it already calls `useSQLiteContext()`), so Task 1.4 changes only the two legacy FS I/O spots; `_layout.tsx` has no nav-bar/SystemUI code, so Task 1.5 is verify-only with optional nav-bar adoption gated on a real Android finding.
- **Placeholders:** none of the "TBD/handle edge cases" kind; where exact config keys are version-sensitive (locales display-name key, `ios.privacyManifests`), the task says to confirm against current docs at execution AND gives the concrete verification (generated iOS project carries the artifact).
- **iOS risk:** all iOS-only surfaces (plugin removals, privacy manifest, localized name) are explicitly re-verified in Phase 5 Task 5.1 Step 2.
```
